name: Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment
description: Adversaries send spearphishing emails with malicious attachments to compromise targets. In Microsoft 365 environments, this manifests as crafted Office documents with macros, ISO/LNK files, or HTML smuggling payloads delivered via Exchange Online. Safe Attachments policies in Defender for Office 365 may detonate the attachment in a sandbox, generating OfficeActivity and EmailEvents telemetry. Successful exploitation typically chains into credential theft or OAuth abuse within the same tenant.
iocs: EmailAttachmentInfo entries with FileType in ('docm','xlsm','iso','lnk','html','zip') and DeliveryAction != 'Blocked'
OfficeActivity Operation 'MailItemsAccessed' shortly after an email with attachment delivery to the same recipient
EmailEvents with DeliveryLocation 'Inbox' and ThreatTypes has 'Malware' after Safe Attachments verdict completes
EmailAttachmentInfo SHA256 matching known malicious hash in ThreatIntelligenceIndicator table
Recipient user subsequently appearing in SigninLogs from an unusual IP within 30 minutes of email receipt
OfficeActivity showing document open (FileAccessed) followed by new process or outbound connection telemetry
Multiple recipients in same tenant receiving identical attachment SHA256 on same day — targeted campaign signal
evasion_variants: Use password-protected ZIP archives to bypass Safe Attachments detonation — password delivered in email body
Embed payload inside ISO or IMG disk image to prevent Mark-of-the-Web propagation on Windows
Use HTML smuggling: attachment is benign HTML that reconstructs executable on client-side via JavaScript Blob
Stage payload as a OneDrive or SharePoint link inside the attachment body so initial file appears clean
Send from a compromised legitimate external domain to pass SPF/DKIM/DMARC and avoid sender-reputation filters
Deliver attachment in multiple parts across separate emails to fragment hash-based detection
Use an LNK shortcut with target set to a LOLBin (mshta, wscript) pointing to a remote payload URL
detection_kql_hints: EmailAttachmentInfo | where FileType in~ ('docm','xlsm','iso','lnk','html') | join kind=leftouter (EmailEvents | where DeliveryAction != 'Blocked') on NetworkMessageId
Correlate EmailEvents RecipientEmailAddress with SigninLogs UserPrincipalName — flag sign-ins within 60 minutes of suspicious attachment delivery
EmailEvents | where ThreatTypes has 'Phish' or ThreatTypes has 'Malware' | summarize count() by SenderFromDomain, bin(Timestamp, 1h)
OfficeActivity | where Operation == 'MailItemsAccessed' | join kind=inner (EmailAttachmentInfo | where FileType in~ ('docm','xlsm','iso','lnk')) on $left.UserId == $right.RecipientEmailAddress
ThreatIntelligenceIndicator | where IndicatorType == 'FileSha256' | join EmailAttachmentInfo on $left.IndicatorValue == $right.SHA256
Flag SenderFromDomain values with DKIM pass but no prior send history in tenant (first-seen sender domain)
